DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
John G. Gherini, Esq. of SEDGWICK (415-781-7900), appearing for defendant / moving party Parker-Hannifin Corporation
Grant E. Walters, Esq. of CLAPPER, PATTI, SCHWEIZER & MASON (415-332-4262), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Tina Lupo, et al.

RULING:
Defendant Parker-Hannifin Corporation's demurrer. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's demurrer is sustained as to all challenged claims with ten days leave to amend. There is insufficient product identification. (Bockrath). For guidance of the parties in the future and without ruling on issues not currently before the court, once there is sufficient allegation of the product(s), the claims for negligence, breach of implied warranty and fraud/failure to warn most likely withstand a demurrer, while the claims for conspiracy most likely do not. As to conspiracy, see Chavers, 107 Cal. App. 4th 606). One Order was signed for both motions in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
